title: Is it Permissible To Play Board Games with Dice (or Chess) in Islam?
stanceSummary: Yasir Qadhi addresses "Is it Permissible To Play Board Games with Dice (or Chess) in Islam" and clarifies the ruling with its limits, evidence, and real-world application. He emphasizes following foundational Islamic principles while recognizing context, hardship, and scholarly nuance when applying the ruling.
alternateQuestions: Is it Permissible To Play Board Games with Dice (or Chess) in Islam?
Is is it permissible to play board games with dice (or chess) in islam halal or haram in Islam?
What is the Islamic ruling on is it permissible to play board games with dice (or chess) in islam?
What does Yasir Qadhi say about is it permissible to play board games with dice (or chess) in islam?
transcript:
## Summary of Yasir Qadhi's Position

In a [2020-09-30 video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X_LDpl58Qh8) (starting at [0:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X_LDpl58Qh8&t=0)), Yasir Qadhi explains the ruling, key conditions, and practical guidance for this question.

- **Question:** Is it Permissible To Play Board Games with Dice (or Chess) in Islam?
- **Core approach:** He grounds the answer in Quran, Sunnah, and established fiqh principles.
- **Practical nuance:** He separates broad legal principles from edge cases and modern contexts.

uh the next question that we have brother munib uh and frankly uh i'm i'm reading brother muniz question but this question has been asked by half a dozen people in the last few weeks is saying that during the lockdown in order to entertain the children to pass the time he and his family are playing board games and sometimes those games involve dice and he says he has come across a fatwa he mentions the website name but we shall not mention it is a very popular website that answers q a about islam and my advice or suggestion is that it might.

have some good but really uh people should stick with the scholars of their own lands or countries even if it's not any but find people within your own locality and ask them sometimes these websites are based from one land and there's a lot of cultural connotation that is not suitable for other lands nonetheless he is saying he found a fatwa from this particular website that is saying that it is haram to play board games with one's family that have dice in them because there's a hadith that forbids this so he's asking this question [Music] so this response um and again.

i remind um the viewers that this is the shortened version uh i know that this is gonna as usual be um you're gonna have refutations and people are gonna have other opinions but this is the reality of subhanallah i'm not the first to say what i'm about to say nonetheless this is the shortened response yes there is a hadith that forbids something called narada shiv that the prophet said whoever plays with narada shir it is as if he has dipped his hands in pigs blood and in pig's flesh okay so this is a hadith that is very explicit now there's.

a number of issues involved over here what exactly is nar dashir and is it applicable to the game or to the dice because again the issue comes how do you interpret this word here the word nar dashir is a persian word and it is actually a reference to the game that has now evolved into backgammon uh there is a rudimentary version of backgammon back then that the persians had invented tashir is a persian word and uh they had this game of backgammon that they would gamble with and so backgammon uh to this day it is sometimes played as something that.

people uh you know bet their money on and the winner basically you know takes takes it all so nada the shield primarily applies to batgame now there are some versions or some reports that might reference the dice as well but the more authentic version is muslim says narada shir and narada is primarily applied for the game of backgammon now some have said that uh the the the the prohibition is to the dice itself and there is some basis that how do you separate the game it is all put uh together as well uh there are some reports about the game.

of chess now there's nothing authentic from the prophecies about chess shot but there are a number of reports from the sahaba and tabirun that that some of them disapproved of chess somewhat rather harshly and it is also reported by the way that some of them did not uh disapprove of chess uh if you look up sunnah one of the great books of hadith there is an entire chapter about what people have said regarding chess and you will find a number of reports that are very harsh ali ibn abi taro for example he he basically said i wish i could throw.

this board game onto your face have you been created to do this he found a group of people all playing chess basically surrounding a match and what that he became irritated and so you have some very strict you know uh statements from the sahaba and tabirun but you also have on the other side some of the tabirun and tabitab whom it is authentically narrated that they would actually play chess and of them he says they would play chess you know in private means it's not something they would publicize it's not something you do in front of everybody but anybody with.

your family or in the privacy of the room and they would just go and you know just a little bit of time to pass to play uh chess and uh even abdul bar he says in his tamheed that it is reported by ikrima that they would play with the nard and say it was asked about playing with the nordashir and said musee of the great great tabery the famous tabari he said if there is no money or gambling involved then there is no problem with it now these are some reports from these great uh tabiron and tabithav your own uh.

i want you to know this is my short response so i'm not going into detail you will find more reports from the sahaba tabernacle that are disapproving so there's no question i'm not trying to hide that but you want a response i'm going to give you a simple response you.

will find a longer list of people who disliked it but these are great scholars who basically said it's okay with certain conditions and uh the position that i'm advocating is that we need to be a little bit more uh pragmatic and take a look at the broader picture those uh respected and great some of them are my teachers and i respect them immensely those that say that all types of games and cards and board games and dice and everything is haram i fully understand i fully sympathize because in their minds they want the ummah to reach the highest level and.

sitting down to play a board game than them sitting down to play chess the stuff that they're watching on television the netflix that they're watching the online entertainment and the whatever tick tock all of these things out there right and so you're going to come and say oh if a father and mother and the children sit down and they play ludo or they play you know uh you know cards or they play a game of chess or whatever amongst themselves just to pass the time that that is haram and forbidden and sinful in the eyes of allah there is no.

to this there is no question that we have a higher purpose in life than just to spend hours and hours playing video games or watching tv or doing something of this nature and not all of these things i mentioned are at the same level some of them are sinful in what we see or what we hear some of them are you know permissible but the muba which is permissible when it is done too much it becomes makru and when it leads to haram it becomes haram right so even the muba it is like salt you add it to the food.

and so my position the fatwa i'm going to give you is that all of these board games and chess and the the dice that is used for non-gambling as for gambling by unanimous consensus when you put money and there are people that are we're talking about real money monopoly is not real money right so we're talking about when you put money and people are are betting and they have games and whatnot there is no question that any type of game that involves money actual money uh and is is based upon the luck of the draw or the luck of the.

cards or the luck of the dice that is absolutely haram there should be no two opinions about that as for when there is no gambling involved then my suggestion my advice is that family should understand that indeed we do have a higher purpose and that we have a problem across the board of wasting too much time in entertainment in movies and all that we do so it's not just the the dice and the the games it is overall we are killing time and we have not been created to kill time we have been created to be productive with our time.

nonetheless if somebody you know you cannot continuously 24 hours a day do something that is absolutely productive the mind needs to take a break the body needs to take a break your children cannot be expected to be you know constantly you're gonna they're gonna play they're gonna do what they're going.
